 {"id":"bd-1cxf","title":"Fix color bleeding and theme application issues","description":"## Problem
Colors appearing in wrong areas, background colors bleeding, inconsistent theme application.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### lipgloss Handles ANSI Resets Correctly

Lipgloss automatically adds ANSI reset codes after styled text. Looking at the codebase:
- All styling goes through lipgloss.Style.Render()
- No manual ANSI code emission found
- Theme colors are applied via semantic styles

### Potential Issues to Verify
1. **Background extending past text** - May be lipgloss Width() setting width wider than content
2. **Plain theme** - Need to verify NO_COLOR and plain theme are truly colorless
3. **Style inheritance** - Check for nested styles that might compound

## Fix Approach

### 1. Audit Background Width Usage
```go
// Potentially problematic - background extends to full width
style := lipgloss.NewStyle().Background(color).Width(w).Render(text)

// Better - background only on text, then pad
styled := lipgloss.NewStyle().Background(color).Render(text)
padded := lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(w).Render(styled)
```

### 2. Verify Plain Theme
```go
func TestPlainThemeNoColors(t *testing.T) {
    old := theme.Current()
    defer theme.SetCurrent(old)

    theme.SetCurrent(theme.Plain)

    styles := NewSemanticStyles(theme.Current())
    rendered := styles.TextSuccess.Render("test")

    // Should contain no ANSI escape codes
    if strings.Contains(rendered, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("Plain theme should not emit ANSI codes")
    }
}
```

### 3. Check NO_COLOR Environment
```go
func TestNoColorEnv(t *testing.T) {
    os.Setenv("NO_COLOR", "1")
    defer os.Unsetenv("NO_COLOR")

    // Force lipgloss to check env
    lipgloss.SetColorProfile(lipgloss.Ascii)

    style := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212"))
    rendered := style.Render("test")

    if strings.Contains(rendered, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("NO_COLOR should disable all colors")
    }
}
```

## E2E Test (VHS)
```tape

Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200

Type "NO_COLOR=1 ntm dashboard --demo-data"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot no_color_mode.png

Type "exit"
Sleep 500ms

Type "ntm dashboard --demo-data"
Sleep 2s
# Verify colors stay within their elements
Screenshot color_boundaries.png
```

 {"id":"bd-20q09","title":"Integration: Support graceful swarm shutdown","description":"## Purpose
Connect AutoRespawner to PromptInjector to automatically inject marching orders after an agent is respawned, maintaining workflow continuity.

## Implementation Details

```go
// RespawnPromptCoordinator ensures prompts are injected after respawn
type RespawnPromptCoordinator struct {
    respawner      *AutoRespawner
    injector       *PromptInjector
    planStore      *SwarmPlanStore  // Stores active plan for prompt lookup
    injectionDelay time.Duration    // Wait for agent to initialize
    logger         *slog.Logger
}

// OnRespawnComplete is called by AutoRespawner after successful respawn
func (c *RespawnPromptCoordinator) OnRespawnComplete(event RespawnCompleteEvent) error {
    c.logger.Info("respawn complete, preparing prompt injection",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "agent_type", event.AgentType,
        "project", event.Project)
    
    // Wait for agent to initialize
    c.logger.Debug("waiting for agent initialization",
        "delay", c.injectionDelay)
    time.Sleep(c.injectionDelay)
    
    // Get the prompt for this pane from the active plan
    prompt := c.planStore.GetPromptForPane(event.SessionPane)
    if prompt == "" {
        // Use default prompt based on project
        prompt = c.generateDefaultPrompt(event)
    }
    
    // Inject the prompt
    paneSpec := PaneSpec{
        Index:     event.PaneIndex,
        Project:   event.Project,
        AgentType: event.AgentType,
    }
    
    if err := c.injector.InjectWithQuirks(event.SessionPane, paneSpec, prompt); err != nil {
        c.logger.Error("post-respawn prompt injection failed",
            "pane", event.SessionPane,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("inject after respawn: %w", err)
    }
    
    c.logger.Info("post-respawn prompt injected",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "prompt_len", len(prompt))
    
    return nil
}

func (c *RespawnPromptCoordinator) generateDefaultPrompt(event RespawnCompleteEvent) string {
    return fmt.Sprintf(`You were respawned after hitting rate limits. 
Continue working on project: %s
Resume where you left off or check the project's open beads for tasks.`, 
        filepath.Base(event.Project))
}

// RespawnCompleteEvent is emitted by AutoRespawner
type RespawnCompleteEvent struct {
    SessionPane string
    PaneIndex   int
    AgentType   string
    Project     string
    RespawnTime time.Time
}
```

## Callback Registration
```go
// In swarm initialization
respawner := NewAutoRespawner(...)
coordinator := NewRespawnPromptCoordinator(respawner, injector, planStore, logger)

// Register callback
respawner.OnComplete(coordinator.OnRespawnComplete)
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log respawn completion receipt
- Log initialization delay
- Log prompt source (plan vs default)
- Log injection success/failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Callback triggered on respawn completion
- [ ] Configurable initialization delay
- [ ] Prompt lookup from active plan
- [ ] Fallback default prompt generation
- [ ] Uses PromptInjector with agent quirks
- [ ] Error handling without blocking respawner","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:40.886893948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:29:13.480989225Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T05:29:13.480848943Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20q09","depends_on_id":"bd-3cdsi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-20q09","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-214u5","title":"Task: Integrate audit logging throughout NTM codebase","description":"## Overview
Add audit log calls at all significant action points in NTM.

## Deliverables
1. Integration points:

   CLI layer (internal/cli/):
   - Log every command execution
   - Include command args (sanitize secrets)
   - Log command result (success/failure)

   Session management (internal/session/):
   - Session creation with config
   - Session termination with reason
   - Agent spawn events

   Message sending (internal/agent/):
   - Prompts sent (preview, not full content)

   - Error conditions

   State changes:
   - Agent state transitions
   - Configuration changes
   - Resource allocation/release

2. Payload guidelines:
   - Never log full prompts (could be huge)
   - Include preview (first 100 chars)
   - Include length, token count estimates
   - Sanitize secrets/API keys

3. Correlation:

   - Thread through all related log entries
   - Enable tracing request flow

4. Performance considerations:
   - Async logging (don't block operations)
   - Batch writes for efficiency
   - Skip logging in test mode (configurable)

## Implementation Notes
- Create helper functions for common patterns
- Use structured logging consistently
- Consider log levels (debug, info, warn, error)

- Spec lives in `toon-format/spec` (v3.0).

- Reliability (no partial/invalid TOON)
- Minimal dependencies for Go build
- Graceful fallback behavior
- Token savings on real NTM payloads (see bd-rmnfk)

// SwarmConfig configures the weighted multi-project agent swarm system.
type SwarmConfig struct {
    // DefaultScanDir is the base directory to scan for projects (e.g., "/dp")
    DefaultScanDir string `toml:"default_scan_dir"`
    
    // Tier thresholds for bead-based allocation
    Tier1Threshold int `toml:"tier1_threshold"` // Default: 400
    Tier2Threshold int `toml:"tier2_threshold"` // Default: 100
    
    // Allocation per tier: {cc, cod, gmi}
    Tier1Allocation AllocationSpec `toml:"tier1_allocation"`
    Tier2Allocation AllocationSpec `toml:"tier2_allocation"`

    // Session structure
    SessionsPerType int `toml:"sessions_per_type"` // Default: 3
    
    // Timing
    StaggerDelayMs int `toml:"stagger_delay_ms"` // Default: 300
    
    // Account rotation
    AutoRotateAccounts bool `toml:"auto_rotate_accounts"`
    
    // Limit detection patterns per agent type
    LimitPatterns map[string][]string `toml:"limit_patterns"`
    
    // Marching orders templates
    MarchingOrders MarchingOrdersConfig `toml:"marching_orders"`
}

type AllocationSpec struct {
    CC  int `toml:"cc"`
    Cod int `toml:"cod"`
    Gmi int `toml:"gmi"`
}

type MarchingOrdersConfig struct {
    Default string `toml:"default"`
    Review  string `toml:"review"`

 {"id":"bd-2dovu","title":"EPIC: Robot Mode Ergonomics + Token Efficiency (TOON + JSON)","description":"## Goal
Make robot mode maximally ergonomic and token‑efficient for AI agents while preserving first‑class JSON support for tooling.

## Rationale
Robot mode is consumed primarily by AI agents (Codex/Claude/Gemini) where tokens and readability directly affect cost and accuracy. JSON is reliable for tooling but verbose; TOON can be far more compact for uniform arrays. We should support both and choose defaults based on evidence.

## Ground Truth
- `RobotResponse` already exists in `internal/robot/types.go` and is the canonical envelope.
- JSON output is currently pretty‑printed (stable and easy to diff) but not token‑efficient.

## Scope
- Evaluate TOON vs JSON for NTM robot payloads (token savings, clarity, failure modes).
- Add a robot output **format selector** (`json`, `toon`, `auto`) with config/env overrides.
- Standardize/validate the robot output envelope (reuse existing struct; no new schema).
- Provide token‑efficient profiles (terse vs full) **without** breaking parseability.
- Add pagination/limits where payloads are large.

## Non‑Goals
- No breaking removal of JSON.
- No dependency on a specific external CLI if not installed.

## Success Criteria
- Agents can request TOON output when beneficial.
- JSON remains first‑class and stable for scripts.
- Robot outputs are consistent and easier for LLMs to parse.
- Unit + E2E tests cover format selector, envelopes, and large payloads.","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-2dyqk","title":"caut: Implement background polling for usage data","description":"Create background goroutine that polls caut for usage data.

## Requirements

1. Poll caut at configured interval (default 60s)
2. Parse JSON output into internal structs
3. Update in-memory cache for dashboard
4. Trigger alerts when thresholds exceeded

## Implementation

```go
package caut

type UsagePoller struct {
    config   *config.CautConfig
    cache    *UsageCache
    interval time.Duration
    stopCh   chan struct{}
    alertCh  chan Alert
}

func (p *UsagePoller) Start() {
    go p.pollLoop()
}

func (p *UsagePoller) pollLoop() {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(p.interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            usage, err := p.fetchUsage()
            if err != nil {
                // Log error, continue
                continue
            }
            p.cache.Update(usage)
            p.checkAlerts(usage)
        case <-p.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (p *UsagePoller) fetchUsage() (*Usage, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("caut", "status", "--json")
    // Parse output
}
```

- internal/integrations/caut/cache.go

 {"id":"bd-58w0z","title":"Parse and validate Robot Probe CLI flags","description":"Parse and validate CLI flags for Robot Probe command.

## Flags

| Flag | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `--method=<M>` | string | keystroke_echo | Probe method to use |
| `--timeout=<MS>` | int | 5000 | How long to wait for response (ms) |
| `--aggressive` | bool | false | Use interrupt_test if keystroke_echo fails |

## Validation

- method must be one of: keystroke_echo, interrupt_test
- timeout must be positive integer, 100-60000 range
- aggressive requires method to be keystroke_echo (or unset)

## Implementation

```go
type RobotProbeFlags struct {
    Method     string
    TimeoutMs  int
    Aggressive bool
}

func parseProbeFlags() (*RobotProbeFlags, error) {
    method := flag.String("method", "keystroke_echo", "Probe method")
    timeout := flag.Int("timeout", 5000, "Response timeout in ms")
    aggressive := flag.Bool("aggressive", false, "Fallback to interrupt_test")
    
    // Validation
    validMethods := []string{"keystroke_echo", "interrupt_test"}
    if !contains(validMethods, *method) {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid method: %s, must be one of %v", *method, validMethods)
    }
    
    if *timeout < 100 || *timeout > 60000 {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("timeout must be 100-60000ms, got %d", *timeout)
    }
    
    if *aggressive && *method != "keystroke_echo" {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("--aggressive only valid with --method=keystroke_echo")
    }
    
    return &RobotProbeFlags{...}, nil
}
```

## Error Output

Invalid flags should return structured error:
```json
{
  "error": true,
  "error_code": "INVALID_FLAGS",
  "message": "invalid method: foo, must be one of [keystroke_echo, interrupt_test]",
  "valid_methods": ["keystroke_echo", "interrupt_test"]
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:44:34.887573672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:38:05.100933769Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:38:05.100885048Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-58w0z","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-5dfye","title":"Task: Redaction spec + pattern catalog","description":"## Goal
Define the canonical set of detection categories + patterns and how they map to user-facing messages and redaction placeholders.

**Explicit stance (v1):** prioritize *secrets*; defer PII (email/phone/SSN/credit-card) behind a future opt-in or stricter safety profile.

## Source of Truth (current implementation)
- Engine: `internal/safety/redaction` (modes: off|warn|redact|block, allowlist support)
- Canonical fixture corpus: `testdata/redaction_fixtures.json` (loaded by `internal/safety/redaction/redaction_test.go`)
- Legacy regex list to migrate away from: `internal/checkpoint/export.go` (`secretPatterns`)

## Canonical Categories (v1)
Use these exact tokens (matches `internal/safety/redaction.Category`):
- `OPENAI_KEY`
- `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
- `AWS_SECRET_KEY`
- `JWT`
- `BEARER_TOKEN`
- `PRIVATE_KEY`
- `DATABASE_URL`
- `PASSWORD`
- `GENERIC_API_KEY`
- `GENERIC_SECRET`

## Pattern Catalog (v1)
The authoritative list is `internal/safety/redaction/patterns.go` (ordered by priority; higher = more specific).

**Provider keys (high priority)**
- `OPENAI_KEY`
  - `sk-...T3BlbkFJ...` (OpenAI base64 marker)
  - `sk-proj-...` (project keys)
- `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
  - `sk-ant-...`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN`
  - `gh[pousr]_...` (classic/oauth/app/user tokens)
  - `github_pat_..._...` (fine-grained PAT)
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
  - `AIza...` (Google API key)

**Cloud credentials**
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
  - `AKIA...` (long-lived)
  - `ASIA...` (STS)
- `AWS_SECRET_KEY`
  - assignment forms containing `aws_secret` / `secret_access_key` / `secret_key`

**Auth tokens**
- `JWT`
  - `eyJ... . eyJ... . ...`
- `BEARER_TOKEN`
  - `bearer <token>` (header or inline)

**Other high-signal secrets**
- `PRIVATE_KEY`
  - `-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----`
- `DATABASE_URL`
  - `postgres|mysql|mongodb|redis://user:pass@host...`

**Generic key/value secrets (lower priority)**
- `PASSWORD`
  - `password|passwd|pwd = ...`
- `GENERIC_API_KEY`
  - `*_api_key|apikey = ...`
- `GENERIC_SECRET`
  - `secret|private_key|token = ...`

### Overlap / precedence strategy
- Prefer provider-specific matches over generic matches.
- If overlaps exist, keep the higher-priority match and drop the lower-priority one.
- This behavior is tested in `internal/safety/redaction/redaction_test.go` (overlap + order invariants).

## Redaction Placeholder Strategy
- Placeholder format: `[REDACTED:{CATEGORY}:{hash8}]`
- `hash8` = first 8 lowercase hex chars of `SHA-256("{CATEGORY}:{MATCH}")`
- Hash is deterministic + fixed-length (does not leak the match length).

## UX / Messaging
- `warn`: surface category + where (prompt vs output vs export) + how to override (allowlist / disable category / per-command flag).
- `block`: fail the operation with a clear `error_code` (robot) and guidance.

## False-Positive Strategy + Allowlist Escape Hatch
- Keep generic patterns low priority.
- Allowlist is regex-based and must suppress findings **by match**, not by category.

## Inventory: legacy checkpoint-export patterns → categories
`internal/checkpoint/export.go` currently redacts by replacing matches with `[REDACTED]`. These should be mapped onto the categories above and routed through the shared engine:
- `(?i)(api[_-]?key|apikey) ...` → `GENERIC_API_KEY`
- `(?i)(secret|password|passwd|pwd) ...` → `PASSWORD` (and/or `GENERIC_SECRET` for non-password tokens)
- `(?i)(token|bearer) ...` / `Authorization: Bearer ...` → `BEARER_TOKEN`
- `AKIA...` → `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
- `aws_secret|aws_access ...` → `AWS_SECRET_KEY` / `AWS_ACCESS_KEY` (split)
- `ghp_...` → `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `sk-...` → `OPENAI_KEY`
- `sk-ant-...` → `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
- `-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----` → `PRIVATE_KEY`

## Fixtures

 {"id":"bd-71cpj","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"## Overview

As a user who frequently uses ntm, I want tab completion so that I can:

- Complete flag names (ntm spawn --c[TAB] -> --cc)
- Complete session names from running sessions
- Complete agent names/IDs for targeted operations

Using cobra's built-in completion generation:
- Add completion subcommand supporting bash, zsh, fish, powershell
- Register custom completion functions for dynamic values (sessions, agents)

- Session names: List active tmux sessions
- Agent IDs: List agents in current/specified session
- Bead IDs: List open beads for assign command
- Agent types: cc, cod, gmi

```bash
# Generate bash completion
ntm completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/ntm

# Generate zsh completion
ntm completion zsh > /usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions/_ntm

# Generate fish completion
ntm completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/ntm.fish
```

- [ ] ntm completion bash outputs valid bash completion script
- [ ] ntm completion zsh outputs valid zsh completion script
- [ ] ntm completion fish outputs valid fish completion script
- [ ] Session names complete dynamically
- [ ] Agent IDs complete dynamically
- [ ] Flag names and values complete correctly

 {"id":"bd-75iqr","title":"Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-spawn for creating ensembles","description":"Implement --robot-ensemble-spawn for creating ensembles.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION \
  --preset=architecture-review \
  --question='Review auth system' \
  --agents=cc=3,cod=2

  --modes=A1,B13,F43 \
  --allow-advanced=true \
  --question='...'
```

### Modifiers (unprefixed)
- `--preset` or `--modes` (one required)
- `--question` (required)
- `--agents` (cc/cod/gmi counts)
- `--assignment` (round-robin|affinity|explicit)
- `--allow-advanced`
- `--budget-total`, `--budget-per-agent`
- `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`
- `--project` (override working dir)

}

 {"id":"bd-77bxi","title":"TOON: Research Go integration approach (CGO vs subprocess)","description":"Evaluate approaches for calling toon_rust from Go code.

## Options

### Option A: Subprocess (Simpler)
Call toon CLI as subprocess:
```go
cmd := exec.Command("toon", "encode")
cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(jsonData)
toonOutput, _ := cmd.Output()
```

Pros: Simple, no CGO, easy updates
Cons: Process overhead per call, latency

### Option B: CGO + Rust FFI (Faster)
Build toon_rust as C-compatible library, call from Go via CGO.

Pros: Near-zero overhead, native speed
Cons: CGO complexity, cross-platform builds, version coupling

### Option C: WASM (Middle Ground)
Compile toon_rust to WASM, run via Go WASM runtime.

Pros: Sandboxed, cross-platform, single binary
Cons: WASM overhead, runtime dependency

## Recommendation

Start with **subprocess** for simplicity. If latency becomes issue, migrate to CGO.

Robot mode commands are not high-frequency (maybe 1-10/second), so subprocess overhead (~5ms) is acceptable.

## Research Tasks
1. Verify toon CLI exists and has encode/decode commands
2. Benchmark subprocess overhead
3. Evaluate if CGO is needed for any use case
4. Check if toon_rust has WASM build

## Deliverable
Decision document with benchmarks and recommendation.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:02:39.102158806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:41:43.597383159Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-77bxi","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7gyss","title":"Task: Ensure common flags consistency across all ntm assign commands","description":"## Overview
Ensure all ntm assign-related commands have consistent common flags.

## Common Flags to Implement
Every ntm command should support:
```bash
--json           # Machine-readable JSON output
-v, --verbose    # Show detailed scoring/decision logs
-q, --quiet      # Suppress non-essential output
--timeout=30s    # Timeout for external calls (bv, br, Agent Mail)
--no-color       # Disable colored output
```

## Agent Type Filter Consistency
Support both styles for agent type filtering:
```bash
# Short form (existing)
--cc-only
--cod-only
--gmi-only

# Long form (new, consistent with --agent in status)
--agent=claude
--agent=codex
--agent=gemini
```

## Commands to Update
- [ ] ntm assign (base command)
- [ ] ntm status --assignments (already has --agent)
- [ ] ntm assign --watch
- [ ] ntm assign --retry
- [ ] ntm assign --reassign
- [ ] ntm assign --clear
- [ ] ntm assign --pane
- [ ] ntm spawn --assign

## Implementation
```go
type CommonFlags struct {
    JSON      bool
    Verbose   int     // -v = 1, -vv = 2
    Quiet     bool
    Timeout   time.Duration
    NoColor   bool
}

func AddCommonFlags(cmd *cobra.Command, flags *CommonFlags) {
    cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&flags.JSON, "json", false, "Output as JSON")
    cmd.Flags().CountVarP(&flags.Verbose, "verbose", "v", "Increase verbosity")
    cmd.Flags().BoolVarP(&flags.Quiet, "quiet", "q", false, "Quiet mode")
    cmd.Flags().DurationVar(&flags.Timeout, "timeout", 30*time.Second, "Command timeout")
    cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&flags.NoColor, "no-color", false, "Disable colors")
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All assign-related commands have common flags
- [ ] Both --cc-only and --agent=claude work
- [ ] --json output is consistent format across commands
- [ ] --verbose increases log detail
- [ ] --quiet suppresses non-error output
- [ ] --timeout affects external calls

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Common flag parsing
- Unit test: Agent filter style equivalence
- Unit test: JSON output format validation","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T22:03:28.850092329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:24:12.774236723Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7gyss","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}
